rules.reason: WP-02 already removed segmentation-only verification and its command surface; WP-06 records that completed deletion without reviving it.
The non-segmentation application case survived WP-02 at its recorded replacement identity and now belongs to the UI leaf.
WP-02 removed label coupling from these application assertions; their recorded replacement identities belong to the UI leaf.
The PNG cases initialize WGPU and were ignored rather than portable deterministic UI evidence; semantic UI remains in the public UI leaf and GPU behavior has its trusted lane.
Application cases requiring an actual GPU are explicit trusted-local evidence and cannot satisfy a hosted PR result.
Renderer cases requiring an actual GPU are explicit trusted-local evidence and cannot satisfy a hosted PR result.
Persistent target-directory report scanning, completion-waiver blessing, and source-unbound external-CI evidence are superseded by exact-invocation evidence and native process results.
These are duplicate libtest discovery records for Criterion benchmark functions; correctness discovery no longer uses --all-targets and the Criterion records remain in performance.
The assertion froze the recursive verify-nightly target array that WP-06 replaces with independent registry-owned lanes.

These assertions owned the superseded source-line limit, deleted evidence commands, stale completion plans, or predecessor workflow-text heuristics; the registry and exact workflow inventory replace them.
Analysis model and pure scientific-operation cases remain ordinary hosted CPU unit evidence.
Normal application tests are headless semantic, state, and interaction evidence owned by the UI leaf.
Core geometry, identity, and state cases remain ordinary hosted CPU unit evidence.
Data scheduling, cancellation, cache, and access cases remain ordinary hosted CPU unit evidence.
Current experimental format acceptance and rejection cases belong to the hosted contract leaf without claiming target-format T1 authority.
Import parser, safety, and source-fixture cases belong to the hosted contract leaf.
Pure CPU renderer math, contracts, and resource-policy cases remain ordinary hosted unit evidence; actual-GPU cases are separate.
Developer-automation implementation cases remain ordinary hosted CPU unit evidence after stale evidence scanners are excluded.
These tests froze legacy per-test arrays and mixed report aggregates for verify-render, verify-ui, and verify-e2e; registry-owned lanes replace that topology.
Criterion is the single representation of these measurements and runs only in the non-PR performance lane.
Seeded fuzzing is explicit bounded deep evidence and is never part of either required PR check.
Install, layout, and headless smoke assertions are package-capability E2 evidence on protected main, not PR correctness tests.
Candidate-controlled product automation is E1 integration support only and cannot satisfy packaged E3 or real-display E4.
The text snapshot is stable headless semantic UI evidence and requires neither WGPU nor a compositor.
The PNG baselines depend on the removed WGPU snapshot cases and are neither portable PR snapshots nor independent product evidence.
WP-06 removes recursive aggregate gates and stale report/waiver/external-evidence authorities rather than renaming or hiding them.
These existing timing and baseline commands are now explicitly registered as trusted fixed-hardware performance evidence and never PR correctness checks.
These checks become nonrecursive implementation phases of the registry-owned policy leaf instead of independent completion authorities.
The development package builder remains a local contributor helper; it does not satisfy the pending main package-capability lane.
The Linux release-package builder remains the registry-owned release assembly command; it is not a package-capability or product-open result by itself.
The ad hoc smoke command is replaced by the registry-owned E1 scenario command and cannot imply E2, E3, or E4.
Product validation remains candidate-controlled E1 support under the registry-owned scenario contract; it cannot imply E2, E3, or E4.
The mixed recursive E2E aggregate had no honest single evidence level; it is deleted while separately registered E1, E2, E3, and E4 lanes remain distinct.
The per-test-array GPU aggregate is replaced by the registry-owned complete trusted-GPU selector and local lane command.
The old mixed semantic/WGPU visual gate is replaced by the portable semantic UI leaf after the ignored PNG snapshot cases are deleted.
The existing code-coverage command is registered as bounded deep diagnostic evidence, distinct from both PR correctness and requirement coverage.
These existing phase comparisons and audits are registered as trusted scientific-acceptance inputs, not required PR checks or self-blessing reports.
These existing pressure and extreme-volume commands are registered in the bounded trusted stress lane, not ordinary hosted correctness feedback.
These are discoverability, local run, and non-authoritative T2 support commands rather than completion evidence.
